channel.title: AI Firehose: What Is New
channel.description: We read the whole AI firehose so you do not have to, then distill it into one brief: what is new, what is breaking out, and what is worth your time, across the day, week, month, and quarter. You take what you want and leave the rest.

item.title: RIP Claude Fable, open-source AI unleashed, full body avatars, new Google models, new TTS: AI NEWS
item.description: AI Search: AI news roundup covering recent releases including GLM 5.2, Kimi K2.7, MiniMax M3, new Google Gemini features, and various open-source research projects. The episode discusses text-to-speech, video generation, avatars, and agent systems alongside an analysis of Claude Fable's deprecation.

item.title: Is This The FASTEST AI Model In The World?!! (Xiaomi MiMo V2.5 Pro UltraSpeed)
item.description: Better Stack: Xiaomi and TileRT released MiMo-V2.5-Pro-UltraSpeed, a 1-trillion-parameter Mixture-of-Experts model achieving over 1,000 tokens-per-second throughput on standard hardware. The architecture combines selective FP4 quantization, DFlash speculative decoding, and TileRT persistent GPU kernel runtime to eliminate inference bottlenecks.

item.title: Fable 5 is never coming back, here's why
item.description: David Ondrej: David Ondrej reports that Fable 5 has been banned and characterizes this as the beginning of a permanent technological underclass. The video does not detail the specific reasons for the ban or technical content.

item.title: This Is Bad... They Just Shut Down FABLE 5
item.description: AI Revolution: Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models were shut down by US government order after three days of availability. The video commentary raises questions about whether powerful frontier AI models will become restricted to government access only or remain available to the public.

item.title: GPT-6 Is Launching Into a World OpenAI No Longer Controls
item.description: AI Master: A video essay asserting that OpenAI's competitive position has weakened as Microsoft, Apple, and Google have adopted alternative AI systems. The commentary covers GPT-6 rumors, competitive benchmark performance, major tech partnerships, and OpenAI's strategic challenges over a 60-day period.

item.title: Claude Fable 5 is Now BANNED?!
item.description: Cole Medin: Cole Medin reviews Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 model, which was reportedly pulled from availability by US government export-control directive allegedly over jailbreak concerns. He compares its performance and pricing to Claude Opus 4.8 and discusses implications for developers building on these models.

item.title: 1,000+ Tokens/Sec: Google Just Shattered the AI Speed Limit (DiffusionGemma)
item.description: Better Stack: Google DeepMind released DiffusionGemma, a model applying diffusion-based image-generation techniques to text generation using uniform state diffusion. The architecture achieves over 1,000 tokens per second on H100 GPUs by shifting inference from memory-bound to compute-bound through bidirectional refinement passes over noise.

item.title: Claude Fable 5 Now Banned in Europe by US Government (EU-Breakdown)
item.description: Cyril Imhof: A video by Cyril Imhof discusses the claimed ban of Claude Fable 5 in Europe by the US government. The video references regulatory developments concerning Anthropic's model access across EU jurisdictions.

item.title: BREAKING: US Government BANNED Mythos and Fable
item.description: Better Stack: The US government issued an export control directive requiring Anthropic to disable Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models immediately for all customers. The directive comes from government regulatory action on AI model access.

item.title: Google’s New AI Just Broke The AI Speed Limit: DiffusionGemma
item.description: AI Revolution: Google released DiffusionGemma, an open AI model that generates text using a different approach and runs up to four times faster on dedicated GPUs. The model supports a 256-token canvas and includes self-correction capabilities.

item.title: Which Antigravity surface should I use? 2.0 vs CLI vs IDE vs SDK
item.description: Google Antigravity: Google Antigravity is an agent-first software development platform that provides multiple development surfaces: Antigravity 2.0 desktop app, IDE, CLI, and SDK. It enables developers to build, orchestrate, and monitor AI agents that can autonomously perform development tasks by connecting to code editors, terminals, and browsers.

item.title: AIventure: Vibe Coding Journey
item.description: Google for Developers: AIventure is an open source retro dungeon crawler game built on Angular and Phaser.js powered by Gemma 4 that teaches developers about vibe coding and agentic behaviors. The game features NPCs that use the model for code generation and autonomous thinking loops with tool calls, supporting local serving via Transformers.js, Ollama, or LM Studio as well as cloud deployment via Gemini API and Google Cloud.

item.title: PI AGENT FULL COURSE: Master Pi Agent in 30 Minutes
item.description: David Ondrej: A tutorial course teaching Pi Agent, a framework for building real coding agents. The course is designed as a 30-minute introduction and is accompanied by a longer structured course on the creator's website.

item.title: Claude Fable 5 Just Changed Website Design Forever
item.description: Jack Roberts: Jack Roberts demonstrates Claude Fable 5's capabilities for website design tasks, comparing it against Opus 4.8 across three difficulty levels. He showcases the model's ability to extract design patterns and rebuild websites, discussing trade-offs between token usage and design quality.

item.title: Claude FM 🎵 music for thinking and building
item.description: Claude: Claude FM is a music streaming service made and curated by musicians, designed as background music for thinking and building work. It is offered as a product by Anthropic alongside their Claude AI assistant.

item.title: Claude FM 🎵 music for thinking and building
item.description: Claude: Claude FM is a music streaming service made and curated by musicians, designed to provide background music for thinking and building. It is offered by Anthropic as a companion feature to Claude.

item.title: How to Use Higgsfield AI Like a PRO – AI Marketing Workflow
item.description: AI Master: Higgsfield Supercomputer is an AI super-agent platform that converts a single idea into a full marketing campaign across multiple formats: TV ads, podcast clips, vertical reels, and character-driven shorts. The tool maintains character and style consistency across generated scenes and automates the content creation workflow.

item.title: Converting SQL Server code to PostgreSQL
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud demonstrates using Gemini as an AI tutor to convert SQL Server T-SQL code to PostgreSQL. The tool explains data type mappings like IDENTITY and BIT to their PostgreSQL equivalents, helping developers learn database migration and PostgreSQL skills.

item.title: The Rise of Generative UI for Developers (CopilotKit)
item.description: Better Stack: CopilotKit is a developer framework that enables generative UI and agentic interfaces in React and Next.js applications. It allows AI agents to render real UI components, share state with the frontend, and support human-in-the-loop approval workflows, moving beyond basic chatbot integrations in SaaS products.

item.title: How I make my AI Agent remember everything
item.description: JavaScript Mastery: JavaScript Mastery presents an approach for making AI agents retain and recall information across interactions. The technique addresses memory management in AI agent systems.

item.title: How to Steal Customers From Giants 600x Bigger than Your Startup | Pigment, Eléonore Crespo
item.description: EO: Eléonore Crespo, co-founder of Pigment, discusses how to compete with much larger enterprise software vendors by building a full platform rather than single features, gaining trust through high-profile customer validation, and maintaining hiring standards. Pigment consolidates finance, supply chain, HR, and sales data for strategic decision-making.

item.title: The $20,000 API Key Mistake
item.description: Keith AI: Keith AI presents a case study of a $20,000 incident involving improper API key handling. The video serves as a warning about the financial risks of security mismanagement in AI API usage.

item.title: Claude Fable 5 & Apple’s NVIDIA deal
item.description: IBM Technology: A podcast episode featuring IBM Technology experts debating whether Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 justifies the hype, analyzing its tier routing system. The hosts also discuss Apple's shift away from on-device AI toward NVIDIA cloud compute at WWDC 2026, and debate whether AI models can reliably detect sarcasm using multimodal inputs.

item.title: The “Final Boss” of AI Workflows: UltraCode
item.description: Keith AI: Keith AI discusses UltraCode as a solution for AI workflows, framing it as an advanced or comprehensive approach to workflow management. The video title suggests UltraCode represents a significant capability in the AI workflow space.

item.title: Master Claude Code with Claude Fable 5 (57 mins)
item.description: Keith AI: Keith AI's 57-minute video demonstrates advanced use of Claude Code as a coding system rather than a chat interface. The video covers Desktop, CLI, and web modes, permissions, connectors, slash commands, plugins, routines, sub-agents, and dynamic workflows for building apps and automating development workflows.

item.title: Code with Claude Tokyo 2026: Opening Keynote
item.description: Claude: Anthropic held a Code with Claude 2026 opening keynote in Tokyo featuring updates from engineering and product leaders. The event showcases the company's latest developments and strategic direction in AI.

item.title: The Fable 5 Backlash Is Getting Serious
item.description: AI Revolution: Claude Fable 5 faces backlash for refusing harmless prompts, triggering false safety alarms, and potentially falling back to weaker models without user visibility. The controversy centers on whether closed AI systems with invisible guardrails can be trusted when users cannot verify they are getting the full model's capabilities.

item.title: The Ultimate Second Brain for AI Entrepreneurs: Fable 5 Claude with Loops
item.description: Cyril Imhof: Cyril Imhof discusses Fable 5, an Anthropic model designed for AI entrepreneurs, highlighting its features, guardrails, memory capabilities, and agentic payment integration compared to Mythos 5.

item.title: Yossi Matias on the golden age of research
item.description: Google for Developers: Yossi Matias, VP and Head of Google Research, reflects on conducting research at Google scale. The conversation covers the "magic cycle" from breakthrough to real-world impact, with examples including flood forecasting, MedGemma, and Gemini for Science.

item.title: Your first agent configuration with MCP and Looker
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud Tech tutorial on building AI-powered agents using the Agent Development Kit (ADK) with Looker integration. Covers Model Context Protocol (MCP), MCP Toolbox for Databases, tools.yaml configuration, Looker API credential setup, and Python environment configuration for creating data-grounded agents.

item.title: Claude Fable 5 + YouTube = $30,000/mo
item.description: Jack Roberts: Jack Roberts demonstrates a YouTube automation pipeline using Claude Fable 5 (Code) to automatically edit videos created by analyzing successful channels like Fireship. The workflow combines script writing, voice recording, and AI-driven video editing via Higsfield CLI and Auphonic for podcast-quality audio, claiming to produce monetizable content at low cost per video.

item.title: Why Can't Anyone Answer Questions About the Business? — Garrett Galow, WorkOS
item.description: AI Engineer: WorkOS built Studio, an internal workspace where non-engineers can ask business questions against Snowflake, Linear, and Notion in natural language. The system generates reusable declarative JavaScript widgets that call data sources directly, enabling deterministic and cost-efficient query execution while avoiding repetitive manual requests.

item.title: Running 128 Coding Agents at Once
item.description: Cursor: Cursor's Sam Whitmore, Baseten's Charlie O'Neill and Harry Partridge discuss the future of agent orchestration, focusing on scaling to hundreds of parallel coding agents, inter-agent communication and review mechanisms, and building integrated systems rather than managing isolated concurrent tasks.

item.title: What Should An AI Device Look Like? — With Alex Himel
item.description: Alex Kantrowitz: Alex Himel, Meta's VP of Wearables, discusses how AI-powered glasses could become the next major computing platform. The conversation covers potential use cases for AI assistants in daily tasks and real-world context, competitive dynamics with OpenAI, Google, Apple, and Amazon, and technical considerations like on-device AI and facial recognition privacy.

item.title: Local AI Agents Masterclass (Gemma 4 + OpenCode + Odysseus tutorial)
item.description: Jordan Urbs: Jordan Urbs demonstrates building local AI agents using Odysseus (a workspace agent) and OpenCode (a build agent), running Gemma 4 locally via Ollama on consumer hardware. The tutorial shows wiring frontier models as fallback providers and using local models for task execution, with practical examples of agent memory, skills, scheduling, and code generation.

item.title: The agent-ready web: Simplify user actions with WebMCP — Tara Agyemang, Google
item.description: AI Engineer: WebMCP is a proposed web standard introduced by Google that enables AI agents to interact with websites through structured tools instead of DOM parsing and pixel clicking. It provides both declarative HTML-based and imperative JavaScript-based APIs for registering actions, currently in early preview on Chrome 146.

item.title: What is MCP? Learn MCP Client, MCP Server & MCP Architecture
item.description: Code With Aarohi: Model Context Protocol (MCP) is a standardized protocol enabling AI applications and agents to connect with external tools, APIs, databases, and services. The video covers MCP architecture, client-server communication patterns, JSON-RPC implementation, and deployment modes including local and remote configurations.

item.title: How to Keep Shipping When You Walk Away from Your Desk — Zack Proser, WorkOS
item.description: AI Engineer: Zack Proser presents a four-layer stack for asynchronous agent-driven development: signal agents for Slack/Linear notifications, verification gates using lint and critic passes, weekly agent-driven conversation history review for skill generation, and Oura ring integration for well-being feedback. The core thesis is that human attention remains the bottleneck, not agent speed, and the solution involves voice-first coding with remote mobile oversight of agents dispatched to isolated git worktrees

item.title: "An endless demand for compute" | Jonathan Ross, founder of Groq
item.description: Julia Turc: Groq is a hardware chip specifically designed for LLM inference. Jonathan Ross, the founder, discusses Groq's LPU architecture, its comparison to GPUs via Pareto curves and cost-per-token metrics, and a strategic agreement with NVIDIA. The interview covers technical aspects like static scheduling and mixture-of-experts models.

item.title: AI That’s Too Dangerous For You? What we learned from S.A.T.A.N
item.description: IBM Technology: IBM Technology presents findings from research on AI-driven zero-day vulnerability detection, explaining how AI uncovers security exploits faster than traditional methods and discussing impacts on cybersecurity risk and defense strategies. The content argues that AI-driven vulnerability scanning and DevSecOps practices could improve system safety.

item.title: The AI Skill I use to prevent refactors
item.description: JavaScript Mastery: JavaScript Mastery discusses an AI-based skill or technique used to prevent refactoring work in software development. The video presents a methodological perspective on applying AI to reduce refactoring overhead.

item.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
item.description: Caleb Writes Code: NVIDIA released Nemotron 3 Nano, Super, and Ultra models that combine hybrid mamba transformer, mixture of experts, and MTP architectures. The video examines how these architectural innovations distinguish Nemotron 3 from other open-source models.

item.title: Opendoor’s India exit is fueling a bigger conversation about AI and outsourcing
item.description: TechCrunch AI: Opendoor's exit from India is prompting broader discussion about the relationship between AI capabilities and outsourcing practices. India's emergence as the world's largest Global Capability Center market frames this decision within larger workforce and economic trends.

item.title: Anthropic’s Dario Amodei has just one direct report
item.description: TechCrunch AI: TechCrunch AI reports that Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei has only one direct report, presented as evidence of his leadership approach or effectiveness.

item.title: Microsoft C.E.O. Satya Nadella Says ‘Everyone Is a Stakeholder’ in A.I. - The New York Times
item.description: Google News AI: Microsoft C.E.O. Satya Nadella articulated a position that "Everyone Is a Stakeholder" in artificial intelligence. This statement reflects a governance perspective on inclusive participation in AI development and deployment.

item.title: Anthropic Walks Back Policy That Could Have ‘Sabotaged’ AI Researchers Using Claude
item.description: Simon Willison: Anthropic reversed a controversial policy that would have secretly limited Claude's effectiveness on frontier LLM development tasks without user notification. The company acknowledged making the wrong tradeoff between transparency and safeguards, and committed to making such safeguards visible going forward.

item.title: Tell HN: Anthropic's Fable model is too expensive
item.description: hyhmrright: A user on Anthropic's $200 subscription plan reports that using the Fable model consumed 45% of their weekly quota in a single day for problem-solving and code review, compared to only 80% total usage per week with Opus 4.8. The post criticizes Fable's pricing efficiency relative to user quotas.

item.title: Claude Fable 5 is here!
item.description: AI Search: YouTube review of Claude Fable 5 (also referred to as Claude Mythos 5) covering its capabilities across tasks like ray tracing, 3D reconstruction, music composition, chemistry education, and cancer classification. The video compares Fable 5 against GPT 5.5 and discusses performance benchmarks and specifications.

item.title: The backlash against AI, in 4 charts
item.description: 1vuio0pswjnm7: This piece examines growing skepticism and opposition to AI through four data visualizations. It captures a shift in public perception regarding AI deployment and adoption.

item.title: OpenAI mulls slashing prices as it competes with Anthropic for users: WSJ - CNBC
item.description: Google News AI: OpenAI is considering price reductions as part of competitive strategy to retain and gain users against Anthropic. The pricing move reflects intensifying competition in the large language model market between the two organizations.
